title: Sign-in blocked: automate SPA redirect URI registration in bootstrap.sh
body:
## Issue description

Signing in on the live site fails immediately with:

```
Sorry, but we're having trouble signing you in.
AADSTS500113: No reply address is registered for the application.
```

Azure Entra ID rejects the MSAL redirect because the `nova-circle-api-<env>` app registration has **no SPA redirect URI configured**. The MSAL client (`client/src/auth/msal-config.ts`, line 33) always uses `window.location.origin` as the `redirectUri`, which is correct for a SPA — but that URL must first be registered in the app registration's reply-URL list before Entra ID will accept the redirect.

`bootstrap.sh`'s `setup_api_app()` function (lines 611–662) creates the app registration and sets the Application ID URI and `requestedAccessTokenVersion=2`, but it does **not** register any SPA platform redirect URIs. The current workaround is documented as a manual step in `docs/cd.md` (lines 169–176) and mentioned in the summary printed at the end of `bootstrap.sh` (lines 925–943). Because it is manual and easy to skip, the live site was deployed without the redirect URI configured, blocking all sign-ins entirely.

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. **Add an `--app-redirect-uri` option to `bootstrap.sh`**
   - Accept the live-site origin URL (e.g. `--app-redirect-uri https://nova-circle.example.com`) as a CLI argument.
   - Store the value in an `APP_REDIRECT_URI` variable alongside the existing `CORS_ORIGIN` variable in the argument-parsing block (lines 129–156).
   - Document the new flag in the `usage()` function and in the `--help` output.

2. **Register redirect URIs automatically inside `setup_api_app()`**
   - Always register `http://localhost:5173` as a SPA redirect URI for non-production environments (`dev`, `staging`) so local development works without any extra steps.
   - Register the operator-supplied `--app-redirect-uri` value when it is non-empty.
   - Use the Azure CLI SPA platform command idempotently — read the current SPA redirect URIs, merge new ones in, and call `az ad app update --id "${API_APP_ID}" --public-client-redirect-uris` or the SPA equivalent so existing URIs are not clobbered:
     ```bash
     az ad app update \
       --id "${API_APP_ID}" \
       --spa-redirect-uris <list>
     ```
   - If no `--app-redirect-uri` is supplied for a production environment, print a clear `warn` message and the Azure Portal deep-link so the operator knows exactly what action to take.

3. **Remove the now-automated step from the manual steps summary**
   - In the end-of-run summary block (lines 912–943) remove or update the bullet that refers to configuring redirect URIs manually.
   - In the file header comment (lines 41–49) update manual step 4 to note that redirect URIs are automated when `--app-redirect-uri` is supplied.

4. **Update `docs/cd.md`**
   - Revise section **2. Configure the API app registration OAuth2 scopes / redirect URIs** (lines 169–176) to explain that `localhost:5173` and the `--app-redirect-uri` value are now registered automatically by `bootstrap.sh`.
   - Keep a manual-fallback paragraph for tenants where the CLI command is not permitted (Application Administrator role unavailable).
   - Add the new `--app-redirect-uri` flag to any bootstrap invocation examples shown in the docs.

5. **No hardcoded live-site URLs** — the redirect URI must come from operator-supplied input only; never embed a default production URL in the script.

6. **Immediate workaround** (until the code change lands): manually add the live-site origin to the app registration in Azure Portal → Entra ID → App Registrations → `nova-circle-api-<env>` → Authentication → Add a platform → Single-page application → Redirect URI = `https://<live-site-origin>`.

---

## Additional notes

- **MSAL config is correct and must not change.** `client/src/auth/msal-config.ts:33` uses `window.location.origin` as `redirectUri`; the fix is entirely on the infrastructure/bootstrap side.
- **`setup_api_app()` location**: `infra/scripts/bootstrap.sh` lines 611–662.
- **Argument parsing block**: `infra/scripts/bootstrap.sh` lines 129–156 — the new `--app-redirect-uri` flag should follow the existing `--cors-origin` pattern.
- **Azure CLI SPA redirect URI command**: The `--spa-redirect-uris` flag on `az ad app update` requires Azure CLI ≥ 2.37. Use `az version` to verify; add a prerequisite check if required.
- **Idempotency**: Read existing SPA redirect URIs first (`az ad app show --id "${API_APP_ID}" --query 'spa.redirectUris' -o tsv`), merge with the new list, and write back the combined deduplicated set to avoid removing URIs added outside the script.
- **Related files**:
  - `infra/scripts/bootstrap.sh` — primary change target
  - `docs/cd.md` — documentation update
  - `client/src/auth/msal-config.ts` — read-only reference; no changes needed

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `bootstrap.sh --help` shows the new `--app-redirect-uri <url>` option with a description.
- [ ] Running `setup_api_app()` for a `dev` or `staging` environment always registers `http://localhost:5173` as a SPA redirect URI on the app registration, even when `--app-redirect-uri` is not supplied.
- [ ] Running `setup_api_app()` with `--app-redirect-uri https://example.com` registers `https://example.com` as a SPA redirect URI in addition to any other URIs.
- [ ] Running the script a second time with the same `--app-redirect-uri` value does not duplicate or remove existing redirect URIs (idempotent).
- [ ] Running `setup_api_app()` for the `prod` environment without `--app-redirect-uri` prints a `WARN` message and the Azure Portal deep-link, and the script continues without failing.
- [ ] The end-of-run manual-steps summary no longer lists configuring redirect URIs as a required manual step when `--app-redirect-uri` was supplied.
- [ ] `docs/cd.md` section 2 is updated to reflect that `localhost:5173` (dev/staging) and the `--app-redirect-uri` value are registered automatically.
- [ ] `docs/cd.md` retains a manual-fallback note for tenants where the CLI command requires elevated permissions.
- [ ] No production URLs are hardcoded in `bootstrap.sh`.
- [ ] The MSAL client code (`client/src/auth/msal-config.ts`) is unchanged.
- [ ] A user who runs `bootstrap.sh --app-redirect-uri https://<live-site>` and then navigates to the live site can sign in successfully without any additional Portal configuration.
